---
title: "I. Introduction"
format:
  pdf:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    geometry: margin=1in
    fontsize: 11pt
    pdf-engine: xelatex
mainfont: "Arial"
---

# I. Introduction


## A. Overview — Watch Video

### 1. Bible Project — The Book of John
 a. [Song Jesus Name above all names](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wk4zVZq6Ojw&list=RDwk4zVZq6Ojw&start_radio=1)

 b. [bibleproject.com/guides/book-of-john 1-12/](https://bibleproject.com/guides/book-of-john/)

 c. [bibleproject.com/guides/book-of-john 13-21/](https://bibleproject.com/videos/john-13-21/)


## B. The Book

### 1. Authorship and Date
 a. Church Fathers' Views (2nd–4th centuries AD) — unanimous attribution to John the Apostle

  • Written in Ephesus late in John's life, after the Synoptic Gospels had circulated

  • Often called a "spiritual Gospel" emphasizing deeper theological truths

  • Irenaeus (c. 180 AD, disciple of Polycarp who knew the apostles): "John, the disciple of the Lord… did himself publish a Gospel during his residence at Ephesus in Asia"

  • Clement of Alexandria (c. 215 AD): urged by friends and inspired by the Spirit to write a "spiritual Gospel" after his oral ministry in Asia

  • Origen (c. 185–254 AD) and Eusebius (c. 260–340 AD): both affirmed apostolic authorship; listed as undisputed in the church canon

  • Also attested by Tertullian, the Muratorian Canon, and Justin Martyr

  • Small dissenting group (the Alogi) rejected it for theological reasons — not the mainstream view

 b. Modern Scholarly Views on Authorship

  • The Gospel is formally anonymous — never names its author

  • Refers to the "disciple whom Jesus loved" as source of testimony ([John 21:20–24](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+21%3A20-24&version=WEB); [13:23](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+13%3A23&version=WEB))

  • Majority consensus: not directly authored by John the Apostle; product of a Johannine community or "school"

  • Reasons: sophisticated Greek style, strong Judean focus, distinct theology vs. Synoptics

  • The Beloved Disciple was a real historical figure; exact identity unknown (candidates: John, Lazarus, a Jerusalem priest)

  • Minority/traditionalist view: Leon Morris, John A.T. Robinson, Richard Bauckham support direct or close connection to John the Apostle

 c. Modern Scholarly Views on Date

  • Broad consensus: c. 90–100 AD — latest of the four canonical Gospels, likely composed in Ephesus

  • Earliest manuscript (P52) dated c. 125–150 AD sets the upper limit

  • Gnostic writers like Heracleon commented on it by the mid-2nd century

  • Minority: some conservatives (Robinson, Wright, Bauckham) argue pre-70 AD based on detailed Jerusalem topography and no explicit reference to Temple's fall

  • Very late 2nd-century dates largely faded after manuscript evidence (e.g., P52 ~125–150 CE) showed the text already existed early in the 2nd century. Today’s critical consensus (including Ehrman) favors late 1st century (~90–100 CE), not mid-2nd.

 d. Passage missing from early manuscripts

  • [John 7:53–8:11](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+7%3A53-8%3A11&version=WEB) (the story of the woman caught in adultery, or Pericope Adulterae) Not present in any Greek manuscripts before the 5th century; style and vocabulary differ from the rest of John. Modern translations usually bracket it or footnote: “The earliest manuscripts do not include 7:53–8:11.”


### 2. Stated Purpose
 a. [John 20:31](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+20%3A31&version=WEB) — "…but these are written so that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that by believing you may have life in his name."


### 3. Distinctives of John
 a. ~80–90% of content is unique — not found in Matthew, Mark, or Luke

 b. Seven "I AM" Statements

  1) [John 6:35](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+6%3A35&version=WEB) — "I am the bread of life"

  2) [John 8:12](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+8%3A12&version=WEB) — "I am the light of the world"

  3) [John 10:7,9](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+10%3A7&version=WEB) — "I am the door/gate"

  4) [John 10:11,14](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+10%3A11&version=WEB) — "I am the good shepherd"

  5) [John 11:25](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+11%3A25&version=WEB) — "I am the resurrection and the life"

  6) [John 14:6](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+14%3A6&version=WEB) — "I am the way, the truth, and the life"

  7) [John 15:1,5](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+15%3A1&version=WEB) — "I am the true vine"

 c. Seven Signs

  1) [John 2:1–11](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+2%3A1-11&version=WEB) — Water into wine (Cana)

  2) [John 4:43–54](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+4%3A43-54&version=WEB) — Healing of the official's son

  3) [John 5:1–15](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+5%3A1-15&version=WEB) — Healing at the pool of Bethesda

  4) [John 6:1–14](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+6%3A1-14&version=WEB) — Feeding of the five thousand

  5) [John 6:16–21](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+6%3A16-21&version=WEB) — Jesus walking on water

  6) [John 9:1–41](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+9%3A1-41&version=WEB) — Healing of the man born blind

  7) [John 11:1–44](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+11%3A1-44&version=WEB) — Raising of Lazarus

 d. Seven Titles of Jesus in John 1

  1) Lamb of God — [John 1:29](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+1%3A29&version=WEB) (declared by John the Baptist)

  2) Rabbi / Teacher — [John 1:38](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+1%3A38&version=WEB) (used by the first disciples)

  3) Messiah / Christ — [John 1:41](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+1%3A41&version=WEB) (proclaimed by Andrew)

  4) Jesus of Nazareth — [John 1:45](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+1%3A45&version=WEB) (identified by Philip)

  5) Son of God — [John 1:49](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+1%3A49&version=WEB) (confessed by Nathanael)

  6) King of Israel — [John 1:49](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+1%3A49&version=WEB) (also confessed by Nathanael)

  7) Son of Man — [John 1:51](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+1%3A51&version=WEB) (Jesus' own self-designation)

 e. Teaching on Holy spirit John 14,16

  1) Holy spirit teaching — [John 14-16](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John%2014%3A14-16%3A15&version=WEB) (covered in future lesson)

 f. High Preistly Prayer John 17

  1) Preistly Prayer — [John 17](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John%2017&version=WEB) (covered in future lesson)


## C. About the Author

### 1. Life of John
 a. Born c. AD 6–15, Galilee (likely Bethsaida or Capernaum); son of Zebedee and Salome; brother of James the apostle

 b. Called among the first disciples; inner circle with Peter and James

 c. The "Beloved Disciple" — remained at the crucifixion; entrusted with Mary's care

 d. Pillar of the Jerusalem church ([Galatians 2:9](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Gal+2%3A9&version=WEB)); founded churches in Asia Minor

 e. Exiled to Patmos under Emperor Domitian (c. AD 95) — wrote Revelation there

 f. Released under Nerva (AD 96–98); returned to Ephesus

 g. Died naturally in Ephesus c. AD 100 — only apostle not martyred


### 2. Christian Tradition
 a. Attributed authorship: Gospel of John, 1–3 John, Revelation

 b. Known for teaching: "Little children, love one another" (Jerome)

 c. Plunged into boiling oil in Rome under Domitian — emerged unharmed; then exiled to Patmos

  • Earliest written reference: Tertullian (c. 200 AD), who mentions it as a well-known miracle

 d. Fled Ephesian bathhouse on seeing the heretic Cerinthus

 e. Disciple chain: John → Polycarp → Irenaeus — key line of apostolic tradition


### 3. John in the Gospels and Acts
 a. Early Ministry

  • Called by the Sea of Galilee with brother James ([Matt 4:21–22](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matt+4%3A21&version=WEB); [Mark 1:19–20](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Mark+1%3A19&version=WEB); [Luke 5:10](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke+5%3A10&version=WEB))

  • Named "Sons of Thunder" (Boanerges) by Jesus ([Mark 3:17](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Mark+3%3A17&version=WEB))

  • Inner circle witness to raising of Jairus' daughter ([Mark 5:37](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Mark+5%3A37&version=WEB); [Luke 8:51](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke+8%3A51&version=WEB))

  • Sent out with the Twelve ([Matt 10](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matt+10%3A1&version=WEB); [Mark 6:7](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Mark+6%3A7&version=WEB); [Luke 9:1](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke+9%3A1&version=WEB))

  • Asked to call down fire on a Samaritan village; Jesus rebukes him ([Luke 9:51–56](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke+9%3A51&version=WEB))

 b. Mid-Ministry

  • Witnesses the Transfiguration with Peter and James ([Matt 17:1–9](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matt+17%3A1&version=WEB); [Mark 9:2–10](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Mark+9%3A2&version=WEB); [Luke 9:28–36](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke+9%3A28&version=WEB))

  • Reports stopping an unauthorized exorcist; Jesus corrects him ([Luke 9:49–50](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke+9%3A49&version=WEB))

  • Requests seats of honor in Christ's kingdom ([Matt 20:20–28](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matt+20%3A20&version=WEB); [Mark 10:35–45](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Mark+10%3A35&version=WEB))

 c. Passion Week

  • Sent with Peter to prepare the Passover meal ([Luke 22:8](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke+22%3A8&version=WEB))

  • The "Beloved Disciple" reclining next to Jesus at the Last Supper ([John 13:23–25](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+13%3A23&version=WEB))

  • Among the three in Gethsemane during Jesus' agony ([Matt 26:36–37](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matt+26%3A36&version=WEB); [Mark 14:32–33](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Mark+14%3A32&version=WEB))

  • Gains access to the high priest's courtyard during Jesus' trial ([John 18:15–16](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+18%3A15&version=WEB))

  • Present at the cross; Jesus entrusts Mary to his care ([John 19:25–27](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+19%3A25&version=WEB))

 d. Resurrection Events

  • Runs with Peter to the empty tomb and believes ([John 20:2–8](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+20%3A2&version=WEB))

  • First to recognize the risen Jesus by the Sea of Galilee ([John 21:7](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+21%3A7&version=WEB))

 e. In the Book of Acts

  • Listed among apostles waiting in the Upper Room ([Acts 1:13](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Acts+1%3A13&version=WEB))

  • Accompanies Peter in healing the lame man at the Temple ([Acts 3:1–11](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Acts+3%3A1&version=WEB))

  • Stands boldly with Peter before the Sanhedrin ([Acts 4:1–22](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Acts+4%3A1&version=WEB))

  • Lays hands on new Samaritan believers with Peter ([Acts 8:14–17](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Acts+8%3A14&version=WEB))

 f. Summary

  • Consistently part of the inner circle (Peter, James, John)

  • Present at key revelation moments: Transfiguration and Resurrection

  • Witness to the full Passion narrative

  • Early leader in the Jerusalem church
